name: sil
description: Turn your OpenClaw into an expert shopping agent.

sil_web_url.description: Override for the sil-web origin (the auth authority — token refresh, registration). Falls back to the SIL_WEB_URL env var, then https://sil.4gpts.com.

sil_api_url.description: Override for the sil-api origin (the domain service — sil_whoami's identity read). Distinct from sil_web_url (sil-web). Falls back to the SIL_API_URL env var, then https://sil-api.4gpts.com. Set this at deploy to pin the sil-api host (or to the same origin as sil_web_url if a single gateway fronts both).

sil_web_url.label: sil-web URL (auth)
sil_web_url.help: The sil-web origin — handles registration and token refresh. Override only for staging or self-hosted deployments.

sil_api_url.label: sil-api URL (identity)
sil_api_url.help: The sil-api origin — sil_whoami reads the user's identity here. Distinct from the sil-web URL. Override for staging or self-hosted deployments.

security.packagingNote: Compiled JavaScript ships under ./dist. register() stays synchronous and opens nothing — it only registers tools. ALL I/O happens inside a tool's execute(): sil_register calls sil-web over the network, writes tokens.json + config.json to the plugin data directory (owner-only, 0600), and arms a bounded background poll timer that stops on the first terminal claim outcome or after the session deadline. sil_whoami reads the stored session token and calls sil-api for the user's identity, refreshing transparently via sil-web on a 401 (at most one refresh + one retry per call, no timer); a confirmed-dead session clears tokens.json. sil_search and sil_product_get read the stored session token and call sil-api's catalog endpoints (search / lookup) with it — read-only on catalog data, no timer, but with the SAME bounded 401 recovery as sil_whoami: on a 401 they refresh ONCE via sil-web (rotating tokens.json) and retry the read ONCE; a second 401 or a dead (invalid_grant) refresh token clears tokens.json and is reported as a terminal re-register hint. A non-401 error writes no token. sil_profile_materialize makes no network call and reads no token: it writes the one sil shopper's SINGLETON behaviour artefacts under a fixed $SIL_DATA_DIR/shopper/ (owner-only, 0600) — a SHARED user_spec.md + profile.json manifest, plus per-domain packs (domain_spec.md + intent_spec.md + playbook.md) minted LAZILY under shopper/domains/<slug>/ on first shop in a niche. The shopper segment is a compile-time constant (NOT caller input — no agentId); the only caller-supplied path segment is the domain slug, validated (lower-kebab, not "main") before any join. Each artefact is written atomically (tmp file → rename over target). Failure guarantee: a FRESH create that fails mid-write is torn down (no partial directory survives); a re-mint of an EXISTING domain is per-file atomic and dir-preserving (NOT a cross-file transaction) — on failure the prior pack is left intact and is never served half-refined (a missing body fails the read closed). The persona is the shopper's host workspace SOUL.md, written by the host CLI (not this plugin, not a sil artefact), and the shopper's host-config wiring is driven separately by the host CLI. sil_profile_get / sil_profile_remove are the local domain lifecycle over the same artefact store, also making no network call and reading no token: get with no domainSlug reads the manifest overview read-only (the shared user_spec + the domains index, the folded listing — an empty store is healthy), get with a domainSlug reads that one domain's domain_spec/intent_spec/playbook bodies + the shared user_spec read-only, and remove deletes exactly one validated shopper/domains/<slug>/ leaf directory (fail-closed on a malformed/traversal/"main" or missing slug — deletes nothing, and never the whole shopper) — the host-wiring half of removal is the host CLI's, not this plugin's. sil_remember is the lightweight per-query memory verb over the same artefact store, also making no network call and reading no token: it appends ONE short typed entry (a fact to the shared user_spec.md, or a taste to the active domain's playbook.md) via a single O_APPEND write (flag "a", owner-only 0600), with NO whole-doc read or re-serialize and NO manifest rewrite. It is fail-closed: append never creates a file (an unknown shopper / unminted domain / missing body returns not_found), and validate-first rejects a blank text, a fact carrying a domain, a taste carrying hard:true, or a malformed/traversal taste domain slug (writing nothing). The remembered text is user content and is never logged. The PKCE verifier is held only in memory and is never written to disk. Tokens and identity PII are never logged.
filesystemScope: $SIL_DATA_DIR (default $XDG_DATA_HOME/sil, else ~/.local/share/sil) — tokens.json, config.json, shopper/{user_spec.md,profile.json} + shopper/domains/<slug>/{domain_spec.md,intent_spec.md,playbook.md} (the ONE sil shopper's singleton SDS behaviour artefacts written by sil_profile_materialize: a SHARED user_spec.md + a profile.json manifest at create, plus per-domain packs minted LAZILY on first shop — the domain spec (domain_spec.md) + intent-spec dimension schema (intent_spec.md) + buying taste (playbook.md). The `shopper` segment is a fixed compile-time constant, not caller input; the domain slug is the only caller-supplied path segment, validated before any join. The persona is the host workspace SOUL.md, not a sil artefact)
